content.text: QAM8775P use 4 PMM8650AU and 1 TPS6594 as PMIC. TPS6594 used as SAIL PMIC.

items: Supplied with valid 3.3V input power
Nine fast transient SMPS
Nine LDO linear regulators
I2C control interface
Dedicated safety island (SAIL) subsystem: Safety monitoring / Build-in self test(BIST) control / Fault detection and fault aggregator / Data storage and event logging
Systematic capability and hardware integrity up to ASIL-D
Supplied with valid 3.3V input power
Five step-down switched-mode power supply (BUCK) regulators
Three low-dropout (LDO) linear regulators with configurable bypass mode
One low-dropout (LDO) linear regulator with low-noise performance
One SPI or two I2C control Interface
Configurable power sequence control in nonvolatile memory (NVM)

rows: Power Good (signal which indicates that the monitored power supply rail(s) is (are) in range)

content.text: Qualcomm deliver whole SIP board to their customer.
There is no document to introduce the connection between SAIL_PMIC and SA8775P.
Qualcomm need explain: which SAIL_PMIC PIN need to control by SAIL E1 software? How to control them? (Can't find any document)

items: PMM8650AU_A SPMI is the master while SA8775P, PMM8650AU_C, PMM8650AU_E, and PMM8650AU_G are the slaves during boot
After successful boot from Main Domain, SA8775P becomes the SPMI master while all PMM8650AUs are the slaves
A dedicated external third-party SAIL PMIC is required to power SA8775P SAIL domain
Among all PMM8650AUs and SAIL PMIC, I2C interface is used for functional safety related communication

content.text: The CDD PMIC driver is intended to provide the following features to control SAIL_PMIC.

items: PMIC Driver shall provide API to change/read cx/mx voltage.
PMIC Driver shall collect all latched error interrupt present on NINT Pin then store them into EFS driver.
Schedule runtime BIST to check functionality of PMIC.
Update NVM Firmware at runtime if needed.
CDD_Pmic driver needs to monitor NINT Pin which is connected to SAILSS from TI_PMIC for NINT Error identification and reporting.

content.text: When there is any error in PMIC HW, the NINT pin will be pulled LOW by TI PMIC.
As AUTOSAR OS doesn’t support GPIO summary Interrupt, we need a configure cyclic task to monitor the NINT state.
The Cycle time of this task should be as small as possible to capture the error and handle it earliest.
CDD_Pmic can configure in SAIL MCAL.

rows: CDD_Pmic_Init function to initialization the PMIC Driver
CDD_Pmic_isDevicePresent is used to return if SAIL PMIC is present
CDD_Pmic_SetEnableDrvPin is used to configure the ENABLE_DRV pin
CDD_Pmic_SetCoreRailVolt is used to configure cx/mx rail to the voltage being passed
CDD_Pmic_GetCoreRailVolt is used to read out current cx/mx rail voltages
CDD_Pmic_SetCornerVolt is used to configure cx/mx rail to the corner being passed
CDD_Pmic_SetResetState is used to configure crash_pad_reg_1 register based on crash state of system
Returns the version information of this module
